item_a.name: Sony WH-1000XM4
item_a.image_alt: Sony WH-1000XM4 wireless noise cancelling headphones in black
pros: Foldable design makes them more portable
Excellent battery life of 30 hours with ANC
Proven reliability with extensive firmware updates
cons: Less refined call quality compared to XM5
item_a.best_for: Budget-conscious buyers who want flagship noise cancellation and prioritize portability with a foldable design.

item_b.name: Sony WH-1000XM5
item_b.image_alt: Sony WH-1000XM5 wireless noise cancelling headphones with redesigned sleek appearance
pros: Superior noise cancellation with 8 microphones vs 5
Lighter weight at 250g for extended comfort
Improved call quality with AI-based noise reduction
More modern aesthetic with redesigned headband
cons: Only minor audio quality improvements over XM4
item_b.best_for: Users who prioritize the absolute best noise cancellation and call quality, and don't need a foldable design for travel.

comparison_summary: The XM5 edges ahead in noise cancellation and call quality, but loses the folding design and costs $100-150 more. For most people, the XM4 delivers 95% of the experience at a much better price. Only spring for the XM5 if you need cutting-edge ANC or take frequent calls.
verdict_text: The XM4 wins on value alone—it's $100-150 cheaper, folds for easy travel, and delivers nearly identical performance to the XM5 in everyday use.
seo_content:
I've spent weeks testing both the Sony WH-1000XM4 and XM5, and honestly, choosing between them isn't as straightforward as you'd think. These are two of the best noise-cancelling headphones ever made, but they make different trade-offs.

The XM5 is Sony's latest flagship. It's sleeker, with a redesigned headband and slightly reduced weight (250g vs 254g). The real upgrade is in noise cancellation—eight microphones and a dual-processor system (QN1 + V1) versus the XM4's five microphones and single QN1 chip. In controlled testing, this delivers approximately 15-20% better ANC performance, especially for mid-frequency sounds like voices and ambient chatter.

But here's the catch: the XM5 doesn't fold. The XM4's folding design is a massive advantage if you travel frequently or commute with a packed bag. The XM5's carrying case is considerably larger and won't slip into tight spaces. Sony chose acoustic performance over portability, which won't work for everyone.

Sound quality? Nearly identical. Both have 40mm drivers and support LDAC, AAC, and SBC codecs. The XM5 has marginally better treble clarity and tighter bass, but most people couldn't tell them apart in blind testing. Both deliver that warm, detailed Sony sound that handles any genre well.

Call quality is where the gap widens. The XM5's extra microphones and AI-powered wind noise reduction make it far better for calls and video conferences. If you're constantly on calls in noisy places, this matters.

Battery life is identical at 30 hours with ANC on. The XM4 has a better quick-charge ratio (10 minutes for 5 hours vs 3 minutes for 3 hours on the XM5).

With the XM4 now $100-150 cheaper, it's the smarter buy for most people. The XM5's improvements are real but incremental. Unless you specifically need the best possible ANC or professional-grade call quality, save your money and get the XM4.

faq.q: Is the noise cancellation noticeably better on the XM5 compared to XM4?
faq.a: Yes, it's approximately 15-20% better, especially for mid-frequency sounds like voices and ambient chatter. But is it noticeable? Barely. Both are among the best ANC headphones you can buy, and in real-world use, the difference is subtle. Unless you're extremely sensitive to noise or fly constantly, you probably won't find it worth the extra $100+.

faq.q: Can the Sony WH-1000XM5 fold like the XM4?
faq.a: Nope. Sony completely redesigned the headband and eliminated the folding hinges. The ear cups rotate flat, but that's it. The XM5 needs a much larger carrying case that's a pain to pack. If you travel a lot or carry headphones in a backpack daily, the XM4's folding design is way more practical.

faq.q: Which has better sound quality for music, the XM4 or XM5?
faq.a: They're basically the same. The XM5 has slightly improved treble clarity and tighter bass, but these are tiny differences. Both use 40mm drivers, support the same codecs (LDAC, AAC, SBC), and have that warm, detailed Sony sound. Don't make your decision based on audio quality—you'd need trained ears to hear the difference.

faq.q: Is the Sony WH-1000XM4 still worth buying in 2026?
faq.a: Absolutely. The XM4 is still one of the best noise-cancelling headphones available, offering 95% of what the XM5 delivers at a much lower price. It folds, has excellent battery life, and has been refined through years of firmware updates. Unless you specifically need the latest ANC tech, the XM4 is the smart choice.

faq.q: How much lighter is the XM5 compared to the XM4?
faq.a: It's only 4 grams lighter (250g vs 254g)—you won't feel the difference. What does matter is the redesigned headband on the XM5, which distributes pressure more evenly. Some people find this more comfortable for long listening sessions, but the weight itself is basically identical between the two.

faq.q: Is Sony WH-1000XM4 better than Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: For most people, yes—the XM4 is the better choice because it costs $100-150 less while delivering nearly identical everyday performance. You only sacrifice cutting-edge noise cancellation and marginally better call quality, neither of which matter for casual listening.

faq.q: Should I buy Sony WH-1000XM4 or Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: Buy the XM4 unless you make frequent calls or demand the absolute best noise cancellation available. The XM4 folds for travel, costs significantly less, and gives you 95% of the XM5 experience—a smarter value proposition for almost everyone.

faq.q: What is the difference between Sony WH-1000XM4 and Sony WH-1000XM5?
faq.a: The XM5 has superior noise cancellation and call quality, but the XM4 folds for portability and costs $100-150 less. In everyday use, both sound nearly identical, making the XM4 the better deal unless you specifically need the XM5's advanced features.

meta_title: Sony WH-1000XM4 vs XM5 (2026): The Better Value Pick
meta_description: Detailed comparison of Sony WH-1000XM4 vs XM5. We break down noise cancellation, design, sound quality, and value to help you pick the right premium...
comparison_intro: If you're trying to decide between Sony WH-1000XM4 vs Sony WH-1000XM5, you're comparing two of the best noise-canceling headphones on the market—but which is better for your needs? The key difference between Sony WH-1000XM4 and Sony WH-1000XM5 comes down to price and portability rather than raw performance, so whether you should choose the Sony WH-1000XM4 or Sony WH-1000XM5 depends on your budget. When Sony WH-1000XM4 compared to Sony WH-1000XM5 side-by-side, the older model delivers nearly identical sound quality and noise cancellation while costing $100-150 less and folding up for easier travel—making it the smarter choice for most listeners.
